hackathon: Torchlight Biosovereignty — Track 2: Individualized Risk Profile

ai_role_declaration.title: Clinical Librarian
ai_role_declaration.definition: AI was used as a domain-knowledge retrieval and annotation layer, NOT for statistical inference.
Every risk score is derived from deterministic Z-score mathematics.
AI contributions are limited to: pathway annotation, clinical context retrieval,
literature cross-referencing, and translation of molecular signals into astronaut-readable language.
what_AI_did: Encoded prior knowledge of SOMA 2024 inter-individual variability patterns into synthetic data perturbation parameters
Annotated each cytokine with published clinical context from spaceflight immunology literature
Mapped cytokine proteins to their cognate gene expression axes (CYTOKINE_GENE_BRIDGE)
Authored clinical pathway notes for immune signaling, DNA damage response, and OXPHOS pathways
Generated astronaut-readable 'Actionable Intelligence' strings from Z-score + literature context
Structured the n=1 statistical warnings based on frequentist statistics limitations
what_AI_did_NOT_do: Modify or adjust any Z-score values
Add post-hoc annotations that were not grounded in published literature
Generate findings that cannot be traced back to a raw data value

literature_references.citation: Overbey EG et al. (2024). Spaceflight affects gene expression in the blood of commercial astronauts. Nature.
literature_references.relevance: Primary SOMA dataset paper. Source for Inspiration4 crew cytokine and transcriptomic response characterization. IP-10/CXCL10 consistently elevated signal; inter-individual variability framework.

literature_references.citation: Crucian BE et al. (2020). Significant Correlations Between Spaceflight-Associated Immune System Dysregulation and Altered Stress Hormone Levels. NPJ Microgravity.
literature_references.relevance: Comprehensive characterization of cytokine dysregulation across multiple ISS crew members. Establishes IL-6, TNF-α, IFN-γ as key space-associated signals.

literature_references.citation: Garrett-Bakelman FE et al. (2019). The NASA Twins Study: A multidimensional analysis of a year-long human spaceflight. Science.
literature_references.relevance: Longitudinal multi-omic spaceflight reference. OXPHOS suppression, DNA damage response, telomere dynamics. Core reference for mitochondrial stress pathway annotation.

literature_references.citation: Bhatt DL et al. (2024). Single-cell transcriptomics of spaceflight immunome reveals lymphocyte subset redistribution and innate immune priming. Cell.
literature_references.relevance: Single-cell resolution of Inspiration4 immune changes. Non-classical monocyte expansion, NK cell activation, T-cell subset shifts.
used_for: Bulk RNA deconvolution warning in honesty_check.py

literature_references.citation: Afshinnekoo E et al. (2021). Fundamental Biological Features of Spaceflight: Advancing the Field to Enable Deep-Space Exploration. Cell.
literature_references.relevance: Comprehensive review of the 7 biological hallmarks of spaceflight. Used as validation framework for the three Health Orbit gauge dimensions.

literature_references.citation: Buchheim JI et al. (2019). Stress related shift of T helper lymphocyte subset distribution during short-duration spaceflight. Front Immunol.
literature_references.relevance: Cortisol-immune axis in short-duration spaceflight missions (relevant to 3-day Inspiration4). IL-1RA compensatory response context.

immune_cytokine_signaling.annotation_source: Overbey et al. 2024 + Crucian et al. 2020
immune_cytokine_signaling.clinical_interpretation: Microgravity-induced sterile inflammation. Activates JAK/STAT3 and NF-κB cascades. IP-10 elevation is the most consistent Inspiration4 signal and likely reflects IFN-γ-driven viral reactivation surveillance.
immune_cytokine_signaling.actionable_threshold: Any single cytokine Z > 3 OR ≥4 inflammatory cytokines Z > 2 simultaneously

dna_damage_response_p53.annotation_source: Garrett-Bakelman et al. 2019 + Afshinnekoo et al. 2021
dna_damage_response_p53.clinical_interpretation: Galactic cosmic ray (GCR) and solar particle event (SPE) exposure drives double-strand break accumulation. p53/GADD45A upregulation is protective and expected in low-Earth orbit. Concern if sustained post-R+1.
dna_damage_response_p53.actionable_threshold: GADD45A or TP53 Z > 3 AND sustained at R+1

mitochondrial_oxphos_stress.annotation_source: Garrett-Bakelman et al. 2019 (Twins Study OXPHOS findings)
mitochondrial_oxphos_stress.clinical_interpretation: Mitochondrial electron transport chain suppression in microgravity. Likely driven by reduced mechanical loading (disuse atrophy in non-locomotor cells) and ROS-mediated complex I destabilization. Monitor for correlation with antioxidant intervention timing.
mitochondrial_oxphos_stress.actionable_threshold: Mean OXPHOS pathway Z < -2 across ≥5 complex subunit genes

validation_approach.internal_consistency: Each synthetic perturbation parameter was cross-validated against published fold-changes in SOMA 2024 supplementary tables before being encoded
validation_approach.cross_layer_concordance: High Confidence signals require concordant direction in both cytokine protein (OSD-575) and gene expression (OSD-570) layers
validation_approach.population_anchor: C001 n=1 Z-scores are contextualized against C002-C004 population distribution where all 4 crew members show the same directional change
validation_approach.honesty_check: A dedicated honesty_check.py script quantifies bootstrap confidence intervals and explicitly flags the ~18% false positive rate at Z>2 with n=3 baselines

transparency_statement: This pipeline makes no claim of clinical diagnostic validity. All Z-scores are
exploratory personal deviation indicators, not FDA-cleared biomarkers.
The 'Clinical Librarian' AI role is strictly annotation and education —
no score has been adjusted, smoothed, or improved by AI post-processing.
The synthetic data used in the absence of real OSD-575/OSD-570 files encodes
published biological knowledge but is NOT a substitute for actual astronaut data.
Replace data/raw/ CSVs with real OSDR downloads before any operational use.

three_gauges.immune_stability: Chosen because adaptive immune dysfunction is the most operationally relevant risk in long-duration spaceflight (infection susceptibility, vaccination efficacy)
three_gauges.inflammatory_load: Chosen because sustained sterile inflammation is the proposed mechanism for multiple spaceflight health outcomes including SANS, muscle wasting, and cognitive changes
three_gauges.recovery_velocity: Chosen because return-to-baseline kinetics predict long-term health outcomes and are a direct measure of physiological resilience

health_orbit_design_rationale.radar_chart_anchor: Gauge = 0 means perfect alignment with personal pre-flight baseline. This is deliberately NOT a population normative anchor — it is the individual's own center.
health_orbit_design_rationale.z_score_to_gauge: Gauge = min(mean(|Z|) / 5.0 × 100, 100). The /5 factor maps ±5σ to the 0-100 scale, treating 5σ deviation as the maximum meaningful perturbation.
